notes: As of late January 2025, Bitcoin is approximately 290 days into the post-4th-halving cycle (halving occurred April 19–20, 2024). BTC dominance remains elevated near 58%, signaling that altcoin season has not yet fully rotated in (Altcoin Season Index ~38, below the 75 threshold). Fear & Greed sits in 'Greed' territory (~72), consistent with a mid-bull-cycle consolidation phase rather than peak euphoria. The Fed held rates at 4.25–4.50% (effective ~4.33%) at its January 2025 FOMC meeting, with markets pricing 1–2 cuts in 2025. M2 YoY growth has re-accelerated modestly to ~3.8%, a historically supportive backdrop for risk assets. Historically, the strongest altcoin outperformance in prior cycles occurred 12–18 months post-halving (roughly Q2–Q4 2025), suggesting the current environment is transitional — BTC leading, with altcoin rotation potentially ahead if macro liquidity continues to expand.
sources: https://www.coingecko.com/en/global-charts — BTC price, dominance, total market cap
https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/M2SL — M2 Money Supply (YoY calculated from latest release)

_data_quality_notes.btc_price_usd: APPROXIMATE — snapshot near time of query; verify live at coingecko.com
_data_quality_notes.total_mc_usd: APPROXIMATE — CoinGecko global page rounds; verify live
_data_quality_notes.altcoin_season_index: APPROXIMATE — Coinglass public page; value fluctuates intraday
_data_quality_notes.m2_yoy_pct: APPROXIMATE — based on most recent FRED M2SL release (Nov/Dec 2024 data); exact figure subject to revision
_data_quality_notes.btc_halving_day: CALCULATED — days from April 19 2024 to January 30 2025 = 286 days; rounded to ~290 accounting for query date uncertainty
